ground_truth.title: How Much Does Pest Control Cost? 2025 Service Price Guide
ground_truth.author: George Schulz
ground_truth.main_content:
Figuring out how much pest control costs can feel tough when ants invade your kitchen or you spot rodent droppings in the attic. In this guide, we'll look at national averages, types of pest services, and regional pricing so you know what to expect in 2025. Our family has run Better Termite and Pest Control since 1968, and in that time our licensed technicians have refined our approach based on real cost data and homeowner needs. I've personally helped hundreds of customers understand their options and find the right service for their budget. Let's break down the numbers.

Professional pest control services vary in scope. A one-time treatment addresses an active infestation, while an annual pest control plan offers routine visits to prevent pests year-round. Across the U.S., general pest control one-time treatment typically ranges from $100 to $260 for a 1,500 sq. ft. home. Annual plans break down to $300–$900 per year for tri-annual treatments, and $40–$70 per visit on monthly programs.

Termite control is one of the most costly services due to the threat to your home's structure. Termite control can range from $200 to $2,500 for a standard subterranean treatment. Annual termite warranties often add $100–$300 per year. Mosquito control costs about $80 to $150 per visit, with a full-season package running $350 to $1,000. Mice extermination averages $150–$300 for initial work, while a rat job might cost $300–$600. Removing a wasp, hornet, or bee nest is often a one-time treatment priced between $150 and $300.

Several elements affect the cost of pest control including severity of infestation, square footage, pest type, region, service frequency, and exclusion work needed. Because prices vary depending on factors like these, always get a personalized quote. While DIY pest control methods can cost $20–$50 for traps and baits, they rarely solve large infestations. Professional treatment often costs more up front than DIY, but it reduces the likelihood of recurring infestations and hidden damages, making it cost-effective long term.

You can choose a one-time pest control visit or an ongoing pest control plan. The TriGuard program bundles termite, rodent, and general pest control under one bill at $39/month. It includes tri-annual visits, targeted seasonal treatments, and unlimited callbacks between services. Organic pest control solutions use botanical oils, diatomaceous earth, and biological controls. These eco-friendly methods often require monthly service, adding 20-30% to annual costs compared to conventional tri-annual treatments.

A pest inspection fee usually runs $50–$100. This covers a licensed technician's time to inspect your home inside and out. At Better Termite, the 78-point home inspection is included with service plans. When you call a reputable pest removal provider, expect a consult with a licensed technician, a 78-point home inspection by a registered tech, initial treatment using products vetted for safety, a clear seasonal plan targeting pests three times per year, and unlimited callbacks at no extra charge if pests return.

In Virginia, Maryland, and D.C., expect termite control at $500–$2,500, mosquito visits at $75–$100 each, rodent jobs at $200–$800, and general pest control at $125–$200 per visit. Regional costs can be slightly above national averages due to higher labor and living costs. Understanding how much pest control costs helps you avoid surprises and budget for the right service. From one-time treatments to year-round plans like TriGuard, the choice depends on your pest problem, property size, and peace of mind.
